docs.title: DataLens AI
docs.text: AI-powered data analytics agent — upload your data, ask questions in plain English, and get SQL queries, visualizations, and insights automatically.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Natural Language to SQL — ask questions like "What are the top 5 products by revenue?" and get instant SQL + results
  • 15+ Auto-Visualizations — bar, line, scatter, pie, heatmap, histogram, KPI cards, and more
  • Smart Data Profiling — automatic column detection, quality scoring, and relationship discovery
  • Zero-Config Analytics — powered by DuckDB, no database setup required
  • Demo Mode — works out of the box with 4 built-in sample datasets, no API keys needed
  • AI Providers — Google Gemini (free tier) or rule-based MockProvider
  • Data Stories — auto-generated narrative reports from your data
  • Export — download results as HTML, PDF, Markdown, JSON, or CSV

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
pip install datalens-ai[streamlit]
datalens demo

Or run the Streamlit app:

streamlit run app/streamlit_app.py

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
graph LR
    A[Upload CSV/Excel] --> B[DuckDB Engine]
    C[Ask Question] --> D[AI Provider]
    D --> E[SQL Generator]
    E --> B
    B --> F[Result Analyzer]
    F --> G[Chart Selector]
    G --> H[Plotly Visualization]
    F --> I[Insight Generator]

docs.title: Sample Datasets
docs.text: Dataset Rows Description E-Commerce Orders 3,000 Products, categories, regions, revenue World Climate 600 Temperature, rainfall, humidity for 10 cities Stock Prices 1,800 Daily OHLCV for 10 major stocks HR Employees 1,000 Salary, performance, satisfaction data

docs.title: DataProfile
docs.text:
Complete statistical profile of a dataset.

from datalens_ai.core.models import DataProfile

profile = DataProfile(
    table_name="orders",
    row_count=3000,
    column_count=10,
)

docs.title: QueryResult
docs.text:
Result of a natural language query.

from datalens_ai.core.models import QueryResult

result = QueryResult(
    question="What is the total revenue?",
    sql_query="SELECT SUM(revenue) FROM orders",
    row_count=1,
    columns=["total"],
    data=[{"total": 150000}],
)
assert result.success  # True when error is None

docs.title: ChartRecommendation
docs.text: Recommended visualization for a query result.

docs.title: DuckDBEngine
docs.text:
from datalens_ai.engine.duckdb_engine import DuckDBEngine

engine = DuckDBEngine()
engine.register_dataframe("orders", df)
result_df, exec_ms = engine.execute_sql("SELECT * FROM orders LIMIT 10")

docs.title: MockProvider
docs.text: Rule-based NL-to-SQL (no API key needed).

docs.title: GeminiProvider
docs.text:
Google Gemini-powered NL-to-SQL (free tier: 15 RPM).

from datalens_ai.ai.gemini_provider import GeminiProvider

provider = GeminiProvider(api_key="your-key")
response = provider.nl_to_sql("total revenue", schema_string)

docs.title: NLToSQLOrchestrator
docs.text:
from datalens_ai.ai.nl_to_sql import NLToSQLOrchestrator

orch = NLToSQLOrchestrator(engine, provider)
result = orch.process("What is the total revenue?", "orders")

docs.title: create_chart
docs.text:
from datalens_ai.visualization.chart_factory import create_chart

fig = create_chart(df, chart_recommendation, title="Revenue by Category")
fig.show()

docs.title: recommend_chart
docs.text:
from datalens_ai.visualization.chart_selector import recommend_chart

rec = recommend_chart(result_shape, hint="bar")

docs.title: v0.1.0 (2026-04-13)
docs.text: Initial release.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Natural language to SQL with MockProvider (zero API keys) and GeminiProvider (free tier)
  • DuckDB-powered in-process analytics engine
  • 15+ auto-generated Plotly visualizations
  • Smart data profiling with quality scoring
  • 4 built-in sample datasets (E-Commerce, Climate, Stocks, HR)
  • Data stories with 5 templates and multi-format export
  • Streamlit app with 5 tabs (Data Explorer, Ask Questions, AI Insights, Data Stories, Schema Explorer)
  • Click CLI with demo, explore, query, list-providers, list-datasets commands
  • HTML, PDF, Markdown, JSON, CSV report export
  • Docker support with HuggingFace Spaces deployment
  • MkDocs Material documentation site
  • CI/CD with GitHub Actions (lint, test, docs, PyPI publish, HF deploy)

docs.title: datalens demo
docs.text:
Run a demo with built-in sample datasets.

datalens demo

docs.title: datalens explore
docs.text:
Profile a data file and show statistics.

datalens explore data.csv

docs.title: datalens query
docs.text:
Ask a question about a data file.

datalens query data.csv "What are the top 5 products by revenue?"

Options:

Flag Description --provider AI provider to use (mock, gemini) --limit Max rows to return

docs.title: datalens list-providers
docs.text:
Show available AI providers and their status.

datalens list-providers

docs.title: datalens list-datasets
docs.text:
Show built-in sample datasets.

datalens list-datasets

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Contributions are welcome! Here's how to get started.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/mekala27-45/datalens-ai.git
cd datalens-ai
pip install -e ".[all,dev,docs]"

docs.title: Development Workflow
docs.text:
1. Create a branch: git checkout -b feature/your-feature
  2. Make changes
  3. Run checks: 
    ruff check src/ tests/
pytest tests/ -v
  4. Commit and push
  5. Open a pull request

docs.title: Code Style
docs.text:
• Formatter: ruff (line length 99)
  • Type hints: use them everywhere
  • Docstrings: for public functions/classes

docs.title: Adding a New Chart Type
docs.text:
1. Add the builder function in visualization/chart_factory.py
  2. Register it in the builders dict
  3. Add selection logic in visualization/chart_selector.py
  4. Add tests

docs.title: Adding a New AI Provider
docs.text:
1. Subclass BaseAIProvider in ai/base.py
  2. Implement nl_to_sql, generate_insights, explain_sql, suggest_questions
  3. Register with @register_provider("name")
  4. Add to config.py provider list

docs.title: From PyPI
docs.text: pip install datalens-ai[streamlit]

docs.title: From Source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/mekala27-45/datalens-ai.git
cd datalens-ai
pip install -e ".[dev,streamlit]"

docs.title: With Docker
docs.text:
docker compose up --build

Then open http://localhost:8501.

docs.title: Streamlit UI
docs.text: streamlit run app/streamlit_app.py

docs.title: CLI
docs.text:
# Demo mode (no API keys)
datalens demo

# Explore a CSV file
datalens explore data.csv

# Ask a question
datalens query data.csv "What are the top 5 products by revenue?"

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: Variable Description Default GEMINI_API_KEY Google Gemini API key (none — uses MockProvider) DATALENS_DEMO_MODE Force demo mode false

docs.title: Demo Mode
docs.text:
DataLens AI works without any API keys in demo mode. It uses a rule-based MockProvider that handles common query patterns (count, top N, average, trend, distribution, etc.).

To use AI-powered features, get a free Google Gemini API key and set it:

export GEMINI_API_KEY=your-key-here

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Type your question in plain English
  2. DataLens AI converts it to SQL using the AI provider
  3. The SQL is validated and executed against DuckDB
  4. Results are displayed with auto-generated charts and insights

docs.title: Aggregations
docs.text:
• "What is the total revenue?"
  • "What is the average salary by department?"
  • "How many orders are there by category?"

docs.title: Rankings
docs.text:
• "What are the top 10 products by revenue?"
  • "Which department has the lowest satisfaction?"

docs.title: Trends
docs.text:
• "How has revenue changed over time?"
  • "What is the monthly trend of temperature?"

docs.title: Distributions
docs.text:
• "What is the distribution of salaries?"
  • "Show me the spread of order amounts"

docs.title: Comparisons
docs.text:
• "Compare revenue across regions"
  • "What is the total by category and region?"

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
• Be specific — "top 5 products by revenue" works better than "best products"
  • Reference column names — the AI can match column names mentioned in your question
  • Follow-up questions — the system remembers previous queries for context
  • Click suggestions — use the suggested question chips for quick queries

docs.title: Mock Provider (Demo Mode)
docs.text: Pattern-matching rules detect query intent (count, top N, average, trend, etc.) and generate appropriate SQL. No API key required.

docs.title: Gemini Provider
docs.text: Uses Google's Gemini model to generate SQL from your question and the table schema. Falls back to MockProvider on failure.

docs.title: What Are Data Stories?
docs.text: Data Stories are auto-generated narrative reports that combine multiple queries, charts, and insights into a cohesive document.

docs.title: Story Templates
docs.text: Template Description Dataset Overview Row counts, distributions, top categories, key stats Trend Analysis How metrics change over time Category Comparison Compare performance across groups Distribution Analysis Understand spread and shape of metrics Correlation Explorer Discover relationships between variables

docs.title: Building a Story
docs.text:
1. Go to the Data Stories tab
  2. Select a template or create a custom story
  3. The system executes each question and assembles the narrative
  4. Download as HTML, PDF, Markdown, or JSON

docs.title: Custom Stories
docs.text:
Provide your own list of questions and a title. Each question becomes a section with:

  • SQL query
  • Data table
  • Auto-generated chart
  • Statistical insights

docs.title: Export Formats
docs.text:
• HTML — styled report with dark theme
  • PDF — printable report (requires xhtml2pdf)
  • Markdown — for documentation or wikis
  • JSON — machine-readable format
  • CSV — raw data export

docs.title: Supported Formats
docs.text: Format Extension Notes CSV .csv Comma-separated values TSV .tsv Tab-separated values Excel .xlsx, .xls Requires openpyxl JSON .json Array of objects Parquet .parquet Columnar format

docs.title: Using Sample Datasets
docs.text: DataLens AI comes with 4 built-in sample datasets. Select one from the sidebar dropdown to get started immediately.

docs.title: Upload Limits
docs.text:
• Maximum file size: 100 MB (configurable)
  • Maximum rows displayed: 10,000 (full dataset is queryable)

docs.title: Data Profiling
docs.text:
After uploading, DataLens AI automatically:

  1. Detects column types — numeric, categorical, datetime, text, boolean
  2. Identifies semantic types — currency, email, URL, phone, country, etc.
  3. Computes statistics — min, max, mean, median, std, quartiles
  4. Scores data quality — completeness, uniqueness, consistency (0-100)
  5. Discovers relationships — correlations between numeric columns
  6. Suggests questions — based on the data structure

docs.title: Auto Chart Selection
docs.text:
DataLens AI automatically picks the best chart type based on:

  • Data shape — number of rows, columns, dimensions, measures
  • Query pattern — single value, time series, ranking, distribution, etc.
  • AI hints — the provider can suggest a chart type

docs.title: Supported Chart Types
docs.text: Chart Best For Bar Category comparisons Horizontal Bar Rankings (top N) Stacked Bar Composition across categories Grouped Bar Multi-measure comparison Line Time series, trends Area Cumulative trends Scatter Correlations, relationships Pie Composition (< 8 categories) Donut Same as pie, with center hole Heatmap Two-dimensional comparisons Histogram Distributions KPI Card Single numeric values Table Raw data display Box Plot Statistical distributions Violin Distribution shape Treemap Hierarchical composition

docs.title: Switching Chart Types
docs.text: Each chart shows alternative chart type suggestions. Click an alternative to switch the visualization.

docs.title: Theme
docs.text:
All charts use a consistent dark theme with the DataLens AI color palette:

  • Indigo, Violet, Pink, Amber, Emerald, Blue, Red, Teal, Orange, Lime
